summary: Joel shares a brief anecdote about an attempted break-in at Fog Creek Software's office, where a burglar apparently fled upon realizing someone was inside. He describes the police response and pivots to a sarcastic note about EarthLink's SEC investigation for allegedly bilking investors, linking it to his earlier writings about the company's questionable ethics.
key_points: Fog Creek Software experienced an attempted burglary, with evidence of forced entry but nothing stolen
Joel takes a sarcastic jab at EarthLink, connecting SEC fraud allegations to his previous criticisms of the company's honesty and integrity
The post is a casual diary-style update mixing personal office anecdotes with industry commentary
money_quotes: They must have run away when they realized I was here.
The police came and searched the place, guns drawn. Very NYPD Blue.
That company is just all about honesty and integrity.
key_insight: A lighthearted diary entry that uses humor to recount an office break-in and takes a parting shot at EarthLink's ethical troubles.
